g.text: What is Secure?

text.data-narration: A secure system is one that is protected against unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.

g.text: A secure system is one that is protected against unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.
Types of Security

text.data-narration: Confidentiality: Protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access

g.text: Confidentiality: Protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access

text.data-narration: Integrity: Ensuring that data is accurate, complete, and not modified without authorization

g.text: Integrity: Ensuring that data is accurate, complete, and not modified without authorization

text.data-narration: Availability: Ensuring that data and services are accessible when required

g.text: Availability: Ensuring that data and services are accessible when required
Key Concepts

text.data-narration: Authentication: Verifying the identity of users or systems

g.text: Authentication: Verifying the identity of users or systems

text.data-narration: Authorization: Controlling access to resources based on user identity and permissions

g.text: Authorization: Controlling access to resources based on user identity and permissions

text.data-narration: Non-repudiation: Ensuring that a sender of a message cannot deny its origin

g.text: Non-repudiation: Ensuring that a sender of a message cannot deny its origin

text.data-narration: Encryption: Protecting data from unauthorized access by converting it into a coded format

g.text: Encryption: Protecting data from unauthorized access by converting it into a coded format
Security Measures

text.data-narration: Firewalls: Blocking unauthorized access to a network or system

g.text: Firewalls: Blocking unauthorized access to a network or system

text.data-narration: Intrusion Detection Systems: Monitoring network traffic for signs of unauthorized access or malicious activity

g.text: Intrusion Detection Systems: Monitoring network traffic for signs of unauthorized access or malicious activity

text.data-narration: Encryption: Protecting data from unauthorized access by converting it into a coded format

g.text: Encryption: Protecting data from unauthorized access by converting it into a coded format

text.data-narration: Access Control: Controlling access to resources based on user identity and permissions

g.text: Access Control: Controlling access to resources based on user identity and permissions

text.data-narration: Security Measures: Protecting a system from cyber-attacks and other forms of malicious activity

g.text: Security Measures: Protecting a system from cyber-attacks and other forms of malicious activity
Benefits of Security

text.data-narration: Implementing security measures can help protect sensitive information from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction

g.text: Implementing security measures can help protect sensitive information from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction

text.data-narration: A secure system ensures the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive information

g.text: A secure system ensures the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive information

text.data-narration: Security measures can also help protect a system from cyber-attacks and other forms of malicious activity

g.text: Security measures can also help protect a system from cyber-attacks and other forms of malicious activity

text.data-narration: In addition, a secure system ensures the availability of its data and services when required

g.text: In addition, a secure system ensures the availability of its data and services when required

text.data-narration: What is Secure? A secure system is one that is protected against unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.

g.text: What is Secure? A secure system is one that is protected against unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.

text.data-narration: A secure system is defined as a system that is free from unauthorized access or attempts to access unauthorized data. It also protects its data from unauthorized modification, deletion, or alteration.

g.text: A secure system is defined as a system that is free from unauthorized access or attempts to access unauthorized data. It also protects its data from unauthorized modification, deletion, or alteration.

text.data-narration: Types of Security A secure system ensures the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive information.

text.data-narration: There are several types of security, including confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Each type of security is crucial in ensuring the protection of sensitive information.

g.text: There are several types of security, including confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Each type of security is crucial in ensuring the protection of sensitive information.
